headline: Hedge-Fund Managers Are Minting Money Off This AI Trade
summary: Bank of America’s stock-trading team summed up recent moves in AI this way in a February note to clients: “Semis > Software has been the YTD (and frankly 2025) trade.”  Analog Century Management, a hedge-fund firm run by physicist Val Zlatev: Its main hedge fund is up about 17% this year through February, people familiar with the matter said.  Positions in semiconductor capital equipment stocks such as Applied Materials and memory-chip makers such as Micron Technology did well.

headline: Micron Jumps 5% on AI Memory Boom — and Analysts Say the Rally Has Room to Run
summary: Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) stock is up 5% in early Friday trading, pushing shares toward $425 as a confluence of bullish analyst calls, a sold-out HBM capacity outlook, and confirmed AI memory supply constraints drive renewed interest in one of the hottest semiconductor names on the market. headline: HP Enterprise confirms memory shortage will last longer than expected
summary: “I keep thinking this has got to end, but we got confirmation this week from HP Enterprise that it’s going to go on for much longer than people think.” That’s Jim Cramer, and the “it” he’s referring to is the global memory shortage that has quietly become one of the most consequential supply stories in ... headline: Applied Materials Raises Quarterly Cash Dividend by 15 Percent
summary: SANTA CLARA, Calif., March 13, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Applied Materials, Inc. today announced that its Board of Directors has approved a 15-percent increase in the quarterly cash dividend, from $0.46 to $0.53 per share, payable on June 11, 2026 to shareholders of record as of May 21, 2026. This marks nine consecutive years of dividend increases. “With the increase announced today, Applied Materials has more than doubled its dividend per share from four years ago,” said Brice Hill, Senior Vice

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Applied Materials (AMAT) After A 127% One Year Rally?
summary: If you are wondering whether Applied Materials is still reasonably priced after a strong run, you are not alone. This article is here to unpack what the current share price might be implying. The stock last closed at US$337.27, with a 7 day return of a 2.7% decline, a 30 day return of 2.5%, a year to date return of 25.4% and a 1 year return of 126.9%. This naturally raises questions about how much optimism is already reflected in the price. Recent coverage has focused on Applied Materials'...
